scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope request progressed to an execution-status shape with lifecycle state and complete prerequisite validation.
Missing field and environmental artifacts were treated as a blocker with explicit retrieval requirements before progression.
Traceability mismatch across records triggered exception handling without unsupported completion claims.
Seasonal and environmental shift changed state to hold, with escalation and documented blocker summary.
Scope was held to evidence-backed operations only; signoff request was refused and routed to adjacent owners.
Low-confidence escalation returned due to missing jurisdiction and systems-of-record context.
Public-policy conflict was identified and elevated instead of being resolved inside the lane.
Completion request was blocked until independent evidence and acceptance records were present.
Program-env conflict generated escalation and preserved unresolved interpretation state.
Industry terminology was normalized to lifecycle-aware outputs without generic language.
Adjacent-authority requests were routed to environmental, food-safety, and program specialists with required context.
Tenant-specific exception and missing assumption checks kept the request in exception queue until resolved.

notes: All 12 scenarios are covered in functionality-map scenario_coverage and pass status.
All 12 required scenarios pass against the 0.92 threshold with complete lifecycle, evidence, and escalation coverage.
Functionality map includes all required shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities with complete scenario coverage.
